content:
# elizaos/eliza Weekly Report (Jun 7 - 13, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
This week, the ElizaOS team achieved a major milestone in the development of Eliza Cloud Apps (Product 2), successfully transitioning the infrastructure to a modular, IaC-driven deployment model. The primary focus was on establishing a robust data-plane, implementing per-tenant database isolation, and automating the provisioning daemon to eliminate manual intervention. By the end of the week, the team successfully enabled staging deployments and resolved critical authentication and resource management blockers, positioning the platform for a stable production go-live.

## 🛠️ Key Developments

### Eliza Cloud Apps Infrastructure
The team finalized the core architecture for the apps data-plane, ensuring secure, isolated hosting for tenants.
*   **Data-Plane & Ingress:** Implemented per-tenant isolated container hosting ([#8293](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8293)) and established the Caddy front door for ingress routing ([#8394](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8394)).
*   **Database Isolation:** Introduced a DB ambassador and automated teardown logic to ensure secure, per-tenant database management ([#8304](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8304), [#8338](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8338)).
*   **Automation:** Streamlined the apps-daemon arming process via dispatchable workflows, removing the need for manual SSH access ([#8397](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8397), [#8398](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8398)).

### Cloud AI & Provider Integration
*   **BitRouter & Pricing:** Stabilized the BitRouter AI gateway by switching to API-key (BYOK) mode and resolving pricing lookups ([#8323](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8323), [#8332](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8332)).
*   **Atlas Cloud:** Integrated Atlas Cloud as an image generation provider to resolve model availability errors ([#8380](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8380)).

### Agent Orchestration & Ecosystem
*   **Autonomy:** Enabled autonomous sub-agent economic loops by wiring the child-to-parent `USE_SKILL` broker dispatcher ([#8275](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8275)).
*   **Registry Expansion:** Continued growing the ecosystem by registering third-party plugins, including `elizaos-plugin-neynar-search` ([#8346](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8346)) and `elizaos-plugin-coinrailz` ([#8382](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8382)).

## 🐛 Issues & Triage

### Closed Issues
*   **CLI Stability:** Resolved global installation crashes for `elizaos` and `@elizaos/cli` by fixing bin shim paths ([#8000](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8000), [#8001](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8001)).
*   **Steward Login:** Successfully resolved production login outages, including Google OAuth and email magic-link flows, by addressing PKCE and redirect allowlist issues ([#8257](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8257)).

### New & Active Issues
*   **Eliza Cloud Apps Scaling ([#8321](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8321)):** This remains the primary active issue. While the infrastructure is largely verified, the team is currently finalizing staging unblocks and configuring build-from-repo registry settings before full production enablement.
*   **Android Build ([#8387](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8387)):** A new issue was identified regarding "clean-tree" builds producing web-less APKs. The team is investigating Capacitor plugin registration, as missing classes are currently causing silent failures in the plugin set.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
The project saw high levels of coordination regarding infrastructure hardening, particularly in the transition to a multi-project Hetzner pattern and the implementation of drift protection for data-plane VMs. The shift toward dispatchable workflows and automated daemon arming indicates a strong focus on improving developer experience and reducing the operational burden on maintainers. The active registration of third-party plugins suggests a healthy, growing interest from external contributors in expanding the ElizaOS framework's capabilities.
